items.title: Create a structured parent-test evidence intake
items.success_signal: The user can record setup time, engagement, quotes, confusion, mess, changes, and repeatability in one concise structured workflow, with no invented observations.
items.baseline: The field-test pack had a free-form log, but no reusable intake with mechanical checks for required observation fields or real-session attestation.
items.hypothesis: A concise reusable form plus a standard-library validator will reduce missing parent-test evidence without encouraging invented observations.
items.expected_effect: Future real sessions can produce reviewable evidence for one of the five declared activities; no live content or tested-status claim changes from this operator action.
items.observation_window: Not started. Evaluate usability after the user submits the first real completed intake.
items.decision: Create intake and validation only; keep every activity untested until real evidence is separately reviewed.
items.result: Added a reusable parent-test evidence intake, a validator for required fields and status/attestation consistency, and a test-pack handoff. Independent review cycle 3 returned PASS after closing incomplete-evidence and template-mode bypasses. No observation, child quote, photo, engagement result, tested status, site file, or public claim was created.
items.notes: Do not mark an activity tested or change a live claim without real user evidence and separate content review.

items.title: Refresh search evidence for the pages Google is already testing
items.success_signal: A fresh read-only baseline identifies which existing page and query deserves the next bounded improvement.
items.notes: Semrush is optional enrichment. Do not create new pages or request indexing merely because a scheduled check is due.

items.title: Improve one evidence-bearing age-4 STEM page
items.success_signal: One existing page becomes more useful and differentiated using real search or parent-test evidence, with generation and link validation green.
items.notes: Never manufacture parent observations, child quotes, photos, or tested status.
